slots.meaning: The child protagonist.
slots.guidance: A single kid-relatable investigator (name or role); curious and fair-minded, never reckless.

slots.meaning: The hero's teammate who investigates and repairs alongside them.
slots.guidance: A loyal, capable teammate; never a source of peril; shares credit and helps repair, not just watch.

slots.meaning: The whole bounded event the story happens inside.
slots.guidance: A single-day school or community event with a public demonstration/judging moment at its close; wonder and fair-play over dread; no lethal or unbreathable hazards.

slots.meaning: The single home-base location the investigation keeps returning to.
slots.guidance: A workroom/clubroom that is normally locked overnight and reachable at lunch for repairs; always safe to enter.

slots.meaning: The hero's own team/club.
slots.guidance: A named kid team the hero belongs to; distinct from the two rival teams.

slots.meaning: The hero's own project/device, found disassembled at the start.
slots.guidance: A harmless, buildable device a kid team could plausibly make; never a source of peril to anyone.

slots.meaning: The adult authority figure who runs the event and hears the resolution.
slots.guidance: A fair, listening adult in charge; not a suspect, a source of calm authority.

slots.meaning: The adult staff member secretly responsible, working on a kind surprise.
slots.guidance: A sympathetic adult with a heartfelt, non-malicious secret project; never the true villain, only mistaken.

slots.meaning: The real, non-malicious cause of the sabotage: an old device woken early with garbled instructions.
slots.guidance: A gentle, helpful machine that only ever tidies/assists; the 'culprit' with no ill intent, never armed or dangerous.

slots.meaning: The recurring cue that cuts off the morning investigation window.
slots.guidance: A routine, survivable school-schedule cue (a bell, a announcement); never itself dangerous.

slots.meaning: The small shareable snack the hero brings and later offers as a peace gesture.
slots.guidance: An ordinary, safe kid-friendly food or treat; edible without concern.

slots.meaning: The first rival team, also sabotaged.
slots.guidance: A friendly-rival kid team, not antagonists.

slots.meaning: The first rival team's device, also disassembled.
slots.guidance: A harmless kid-built device.

slots.meaning: The second rival team, also sabotaged.
slots.guidance: A friendly-rival kid team, not antagonists.

slots.meaning: The second rival team's device, also disassembled.
slots.guidance: A harmless kid-built device.

slots.meaning: The first red-herring suspect: a rival-team kid with an innocent, embarrassing secret.
slots.guidance: A nervous but decent kid; his secret is small and non-malicious (a borrowed part, a late errand).

slots.meaning: This suspect's own teammate, who unknowingly confirms his alibi.
slots.guidance: A candid, unworried teammate.

slots.meaning: The second red-herring suspect: a much younger sibling who witnessed something real out of fear of being blamed.
slots.guidance: A young, frightened but honest child; never actually responsible, needs gentle handling not accusation.

slots.meaning: This suspect's older sibling, one of the hero's own teammates.
slots.guidance: A protective older sibling who helps coax the truth out gently.

slots.meaning: The storage space inside the workshop where the real culprit is eventually found.
slots.guidance: An ordinary, safe storage space; never a source of physical danger.

slots.meaning: The recurring physical clue (unidentified marks) that eventually reveals wheels, not feet.
slots.guidance: A benign physical trace; harmless once explained.

slots.meaning: The venue for the assembly and the final demonstrations.
slots.guidance: A large, safe public gathering space.

slots.meaning: The garbled instruction card that caused the old device's confused behavior.
slots.guidance: A harmless instruction medium; the 'bug' behind the mystery, not a weapon.

slots.meaning: The custodian's written record explaining the secret project.
slots.guidance: A plain written record; the mechanism by which the truth is confirmed.

slots.meaning: The stencil label words found on the sorted bins.
slots.guidance: Short, blocky category words; a physical-evidence detail, not a taunt or threat.

slots.meaning: The single piece of physical evidence the hero pockets from the bins clue.
slots.guidance: A small, harmless item.

slots.meaning: The custodian's own workroom, glimpsed as a secondary clue location.
slots.guidance: A staff workroom; safe, if a little off-limits to kids.

slots.meaning: The physical chewed card found in the charging dock, later identified as the garbled instruction.
slots.guidance: A small inert artifact.

slots.meaning: The risky manual-reteach control on the old device.
slots.guidance: A control that is safe to use but easy to use wrong; never itself hazardous.

slots.meaning: The safe emergency shutoff on the old device.
slots.guidance: A simple, safe stop control; using it is always harmless, just costs time.

slots.meaning: The celebratory prop offered to the old device for the finale.
slots.guidance: A safe, fun party prop.

slots.meaning: The spare part a red-herring suspect is nervously carrying.
slots.guidance: An ordinary spare mechanical part, not a weapon.

slots.meaning: The item the young witness clutches protectively.
slots.guidance: A comforting personal item, e.g. a book of drawings.

slots.meaning: The empty docking station found in the closet.
slots.guidance: An ordinary piece of equipment furniture; inert.

slots.meaning: The young witness's good-luck drawing, dropped and later found put away with care.
slots.guidance: A small, sweet handmade keepsake.

slots.meaning: The old device's fanfare/song, sung along by the crowd in more than one ending.
slots.guidance: A warm, communal, harmless tradition.

slots.meaning: Ending title: the old device's honored second debut (completion).
slots.guidance: Positive; names the old device's redemption. Names ending e_honor.

slots.meaning: The special prize won for the steady-hands repair-and-run ending (success).
slots.guidance: Positive; a judge-created recognition, not a top prize. Names ending e_grit.

slots.meaning: The invented prize for the live-repair-on-stage ending (discovery).
slots.guidance: Positive; celebrates problem-solving over a clean run. Names ending e_showfix.

slots.meaning: The prize for the hero-and-old-device duet ending (success).
slots.guidance: Positive; rewards a joyful shared performance. Names ending e_duo.

slots.meaning: The honorary status the old device is given in the duet ending (success).
slots.guidance: Positive; a warm honorary recognition. Names ending e_duo.

slots.meaning: The shared prize for the all-three-teams-together finale (success).
slots.guidance: Positive; celebrates cooperation among former rivals. Names ending e_team.
